mnemonics: REF: Reflexive, Every element is related to itself. Symmetric, Flip the pair and it's still related. Transitive, If a~b and b~c, then a~c.
PIE: Partition Is Equivalence. Every partition of a set defines an equivalence relation, and every equivalence relation defines a partition.
keyPoints: An equivalence relation on a set A is a relation that is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.
The equivalence class of an element a in A is the set of all elements in A that are related to a. Denoted [a].
The set of all equivalence classes forms a partition of the original set A. This is called the quotient set, A/~.
commonMistakes: Confusing symmetric property (if a~b then b~a) with the transitive property (if a~b and b~c then a~c).
Assuming that if a relation is both reflexive and symmetric, it must be transitive. This is not true; it must satisfy all three properties.
Forgetting that the equivalence classes of a relation must be non-empty, disjoint, and their union must be the entire set A for it to be a partition.
